contentBlocks.original:
किं ज्योतिस्तवभानुमानहनि मे रात्रौ प्रदीपादिकं
स्यादेवं रविदीपदर्शनविधौ किं ज्योतिराख्याहि मे ।
चक्षुस्तस्य निमीलनादिसमये किं धीर्धियो दर्शने
किं तत्राहमतो भवान्परमकं ज्योतिस्तदस्मि प्रभो ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kiṃ jyotistavabhānumānahani me rātrau pradīpādikaṃ
syādevaṃ ravidīpadarśanavidhau kiṃ jyotirākhyāhi me .
cakṣustasya nimīlanādisamaye kiṃ dhīrdhiyo darśane
kiṃ tatrāhamato bhavānparamakaṃ jyotistadasmi prabho ..
contentBlocks.translation:
Guru: What provides you with light?
Sishya: By day, the sun, by night, a lamp.
Guru: What is the light that sees these lights?
Sishya: The eye.
Guru: What is the light that illumines the eye?
Sishya: The intellect.
Guru: What is it that knows the intellect?
Sishya: It is the "I".
Guru: Therefore, you are the light of lights.
Sishya: Truly, I am That. (I realize that I am)
contentBlocks.meanings: किं (kiṃ) – what; ज्योतिः (jyotiḥ) – light; तव (tava) – your; भानुमानहनि (bhānumānahani) – sun during the day (bhānumān:sun | ahani:during the day); मे (me) – my; रात्रौ (rātrau) – at night; प्रदीपादिकं (pradīpādikaṃ) – lamp and others (pradīpa:lamp | ādikaṃ:and others); स्यात् (syāt) – may be; एवं (evaṃ) – thus; रविदीपदर्शनविधौ (ravidīpadarśanavidhau) – in seeing sun and lamp (ravi:sun | dīpa:lamp | darśana:seeing | vidhau:in the method); किं (kiṃ) – what; ज्योतिः (jyotiḥ) – light; आख्याहि (ākhyāhi) – tell; मे (me) – me; चक्षुः (cakṣuḥ) – eye; तस्य (tasya) – its; निमीलनादिसमये (nimīlanādisamaye) – during closing and others (nimīlana:closing | ādi:and others | samaye:during); किं (kiṃ) – what; धीः (dhīḥ) – intellect; धियः (dhiyaḥ) – of intellect; दर्शने (darśane) – in seeing; किं (kiṃ) – what; तत्र (tatra) – there; अहम् (aham) – I; अतः (ataḥ) – therefore; भवान् (bhavān) – you; परमकं (paramakaṃ) – supreme; ज्योतिः (jyotiḥ) – light; तदस्मि (tadasmi) – I am that (tat:that | asmi:I am); प्रभो (prabho) – O Lord;
